claim: A new domain MUST be founded general-before-specific, not bottom-up from requirements, in 8 steps (the same authored-executable-specification canvas PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md Section 8 applies to a whole domain): (1) purpose, stakeholders, terminology MUST land first -- manifest purpose/goals plus Stakeholders plus Axes; (2) Process and its scenarios MUST land BEFORE any EntityType -- a Process node naming stages, roles, and drives_entities; (3) the object model, authored by hand in spec/model/ (all aggregates/value-objects and their fields, general before any one model's detail) MUST land BEFORE any operation; (4) operations, policies, and invariants, authored by hand in spec/application/ and spec/policy/, MUST land BEFORE any test; (5) executable tests, authored by hand, MUST land BEFORE the Requirements they prove; (6) Requirements, each linked to that authored code and those tests via implemented_by/verified_by, MUST land BEFORE Assumptions/Conflicts; (7) Assumptions and Conflicts MUST land BEFORE the final step; (8) generated documentation (REQUIREMENTS/PIPELINE/MODELS/TRACEABILITY/COVERAGE.md) and a coverage audit close the founding sequence. Each step MUST close with a regeneration (`hotam gen-spec`) and a check of the overview projection (PIPELINE.md, R-domain-overview-projection) before the next step opens. The diff against the domain's original source prose MUST run AFTER step 1 (purpose/stakeholders/terminology), NEVER only at the end of the founding sequence.
why: Resolver finding this session (task #203, dogfooded on the `prat` pilot domain, PRAT-hotam/domains/prat): the methodology already defines WHAT a domain needs (manifest, Process, Axes, EntityTypes, Requirements, Assumptions/Conflicts -- each with its own SETTLED requirements and check_* invariants) and HOW to add each node kind (the six-step mediation loop, R-... per node kind), but nowhere states in WHAT ORDER a brand-new domain should be founded. The `prat` pilot paid for this gap concretely: its Requirements were landed before any Process node existed (the Process-node-for-stages pattern only arrived later, task #199/#200, see R-process-aspect-first's successors and R-domain-overview-projection/task #201), and its manifest stayed empty for an extended stretch (partially fixed by task #200; full fix tracked as tasks #210/#211, not yet done at authorship time). The compounding failure this produced: an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could NOT answer from the graph + generated docs alone and had to fall back to reading the original prose document directly (diagnosed and partially closed by task #205 / R-domain-overview-projection, commit 7520545, docs/GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md, then docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §9) -- a direct, observed consequence of building artifacts and rules before the skeleton (Process/manifest) existed to anchor them. This requirement originally named the wave order (skeleton -> artifacts -> rules -> tensions -> data, EntityInstances as wave 4). UPDATED task #234 (PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md, resolver decision 2026-07-16, whole-file dated same session as the authored-spec discipline's own founding turnover): the framework itself pivoted from a code-generation discipline (an EntityType wave that produced generated Go structs/lifecycle methods, now retired -- see docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §8 'History') to an authored-executable-specification discipline where an agent writes the domain's model/behavior/tests BY HAND (spec/model, spec/application, spec/policy, spec/tests) and Requirements carry implemented_by/verified_by links into that authored code (R-spec-link-embodied-vs-proven, task #223/#235). The old wave-4 EntityInstance step and the wave-1-as-generated-artifacts framing were defects of the code-generation era (I-2/I-3/I-4 from task #207's own retrospective) that dissolve naturally once founding order is re-expressed as the 8-step authored canvas PLAN §8 defines: object model (by hand) -> operations/policies/invariants (by hand) -> executable tests (by hand) -> Requirements linked to that code+tests -> Assumptions/Conflicts -> generated documentation. This UPDATE changes the claim's STRUCTURE (5 waves -> 8 steps, EntityType-generation language -> authored spec/ language) but preserves the original claim's SUBSTANCE unchanged: general-before-specific, skeleton (purpose/Process/Axes) before detail, regeneration+PIPELINE.md check at every step boundary, source-prose diff right after the skeleton step -- not a new requirement, a reworded restatement of the same discipline for the authored-spec era, refines-related to R-domain-overview-projection unchanged (see relations) and now sibling to R-authored-spec-layer-progression (task #235), which already explicitly refines this requirement's general-before-specific principle one layer down, into how authored spec/ CODE itself is built once a domain's Requirements already exist. Correctly landed as PROSE/feature-blocked (R-speculative-aspects-frozen's sibling pattern, the same honest-roadmap-debt shape this requirement always used): no domain-founding-wave-sequencer tool exists yet to mechanically enforce step ordering -- the claim is real and SETTLED today as operator discipline (the six-step mediation loop enforces WHAT and HOW per proposal; this requirement adds the missing WHEN/ORDER dimension a human or agent founding a domain must follow by hand until the sequencer exists), the enforcer is honestly not-yet-built roadmap debt, not a silent omission.
evidence: the `prat` pilot domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) landed Requirements before any Process node existed for its own staged methodology -- the Process-node-for-stages pattern only arrived later via tasks #199/#200, the `prat` pilot's manifest stayed empty for an extended stretch, partially fixed by task #200, full fix tracked as not-yet-executed follow-on tasks #210/#211, an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could not answer from the graph + generated docs alone, diagnosed as R-domain-overview-projection (task #205, commit 7520545, docs/GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §7, now docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §9)

task #234: founding-order text updated from the 5-wave code-generation canvas to the 8-step authored-executable-specification canvas across internal/generator/claudemd_static.go (crystal), cmd/hotam/init_cmd.go (README scaffold template), and this claim, per PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §8 and the retirement of hotam gen-code (docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §8 'History')

task #234 follow-up: corrected a double-escaped \u00a7 (literal backslash-u-0-0-a-7 text) that leaked into the prior UPDATE's why/evidence fields from an authoring mistake -- fixed to render the real § section-mark character
